verses.text: Publicans and sinners draw near; the Pharisees murmur, 'This man receiveth sinners.' The charge and the glory. What they scorn is the very heart of the gospel.
Publicans and sinners draw near; the Pharisees murmur, 'This man receiveth sinners.' The charge and the glory. What they scorn is the very heart of the gospel.
He leaves the ninety-nine to seek one lost sheep; 'joy... in heaven over one sinner that repenteth.' Seeking love. God rejoices over each one found.
He leaves the ninety-nine to seek one lost sheep; 'joy... in heaven over one sinner that repenteth.' Seeking love. God rejoices over each one found.
He leaves the ninety-nine to seek one lost sheep; 'joy... in heaven over one sinner that repenteth.' Seeking love. God rejoices over each one found.
He leaves the ninety-nine to seek one lost sheep; 'joy... in heaven over one sinner that repenteth.' Seeking love. God rejoices over each one found.
He leaves the ninety-nine to seek one lost sheep; 'joy... in heaven over one sinner that repenteth.' Seeking love. God rejoices over each one found.
A woman sweeps for one lost coin; 'joy in the presence of the angels' over one repenting. Diligent search. Heaven celebrates every soul reclaimed.
A woman sweeps for one lost coin; 'joy in the presence of the angels' over one repenting. Diligent search. Heaven celebrates every soul reclaimed.
A woman sweeps for one lost coin; 'joy in the presence of the angels' over one repenting. Diligent search. Heaven celebrates every soul reclaimed.
A younger son takes his portion and wastes it in riotous living. Wandering. The sinner squanders God's gifts far from home.
A younger son takes his portion and wastes it in riotous living. Wandering. The sinner squanders God's gifts far from home.
A younger son takes his portion and wastes it in riotous living. Wandering. The sinner squanders God's gifts far from home.
A famine comes; he feeds swine and would eat their husks; 'no man gave unto him.' Bitter end. Sin leaves the soul starving and friendless.
A famine comes; he feeds swine and would eat their husks; 'no man gave unto him.' Bitter end. Sin leaves the soul starving and friendless.
A famine comes; he feeds swine and would eat their husks; 'no man gave unto him.' Bitter end. Sin leaves the soul starving and friendless.
'He came to himself': I will arise and go to my father, and say, I have sinned. Awakening. Repentance begins with seeing one's true state.
'He came to himself': I will arise and go to my father, and say, I have sinned. Awakening. Repentance begins with seeing one's true state.
'He came to himself': I will arise and go to my father, and say, I have sinned. Awakening. Repentance begins with seeing one's true state.
'When he was yet a great way off, his father... ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.' Running grace. God meets the returning sinner with eager love.
'When he was yet a great way off, his father... ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.' Running grace. God meets the returning sinner with eager love.
The best robe, ring, and fatted calf; 'this my son was dead, and is alive again.' Full welcome. The penitent is restored to a son's place and joy.
The best robe, ring, and fatted calf; 'this my son was dead, and is alive again.' Full welcome. The penitent is restored to a son's place and joy.
The best robe, ring, and fatted calf; 'this my son was dead, and is alive again.' Full welcome. The penitent is restored to a son's place and joy.
The elder son, hearing the music, is angry and will not go in. Cold heart. Self-righteousness cannot share the joy of grace.
The elder son, hearing the music, is angry and will not go in. Cold heart. Self-righteousness cannot share the joy of grace.
The elder son, hearing the music, is angry and will not go in. Cold heart. Self-righteousness cannot share the joy of grace.
The elder son, hearing the music, is angry and will not go in. Cold heart. Self-righteousness cannot share the joy of grace.
I served thee, yet thou never gavest me a kid; but this thy son devoured thy living. Resentment. He measures by merit, not mercy.
I served thee, yet thou never gavest me a kid; but this thy son devoured thy living. Resentment. He measures by merit, not mercy.
'Son, thou art ever with me'; it was right to be glad, for thy brother was dead and is alive. Father's plea. Grace invites even the grudging to rejoice.
